title: Diagnose Claude upstream rate limiting
description: User reports Claude API Error: Server is temporarily limiting requests (not usage limit) after headroom route was restored. Verify whether this is upstream Anthropic rate limiting, inherited proxy env, or local proxy retry behavior.

title: Make sidebar more minimal
description: Update the app sidebar toward the provided minimal reference: compact dark workspace list, subdued chrome, status dots, badges, and active row treatment.
notes: Implemented scoped sidebar visual updates and added per-project custom emoji icon support via Project Settings. Icons are preserved with automatic fallback when no custom emoji is set.
close_reason: Completed minimal sidebar styling and per-project custom emoji icons; npm run build passes. Requested external CMake build could not run because /home/user/Programs/Pure Harmony Midi Editor/plugin/build does not exist.

title: Fix stale opencode TUI render on tab/workspace switch
description: Updated scope: only opencode (not Claude Code) has the stale-render bug on tab/workspace switch. Fix already applied: transient SIGWINCH (rows-1 then back) gated on backend === 'opencode' in src/renderer/components/terminal/Terminal.tsx isActive useEffect (lines 150-166).

title: Restore the exact live Hermes session for each workspace tab
description: Claude-Terminal restored a stale Hermes session (donation nudge) rather than the conversation active in the tab when the app closed. Inspect workspace persistence/session ID propagation and fix without title-based guessing.
close_reason: Hermes active session identity now follows the TUI's authoritative active-session file after /resume and is persisted to the owning workspace tab; regression tests and production build pass.

comments.author: DonutsDelivery
comments.text: Confirmed failure mode: Hermes workspace tabs can be rebound by session discovery rather than treating the persisted session ID as authoritative. For exact restoration, a saved Hermes ID must be passed straight through to --resume even when discovery lists different/newer sessions or temporarily cannot find it. Implementing that invariant; no live app restart.

comments.author: DonutsDelivery
comments.text: Implemented strict Hermes restoration: when a tab has a persisted Hermes sessionId, workspace restoration passes it directly to --resume and bypasses discovery/title/recency fallback. Session polling also no longer replaces an existing Hermes ID merely because discovery shows another or newer session. Added regression coverage for discovery returning a newer unrelated session.

comments.author: DonutsDelivery
comments.text: Focused regression test + production build pass. Per user instruction, no live-app restart will be performed; the persistence fix takes effect on their next intentional restart. Investigating the two new Hermes session IDs created immediately before the last restart to help recover the intended conversation manually.

comments.author: DonutsDelivery
comments.text: Recovery evidence: two PTY-ID-named tmux sessions survived the restart despite not being represented in workspace persistence: ct-hermes-b212b243-1dce-4863-b2fb-a8524fa7fa66 (idle crash-fix work) and ct-hermes-6062af57-ae94-4e01-b201-38091c3d43cf (active updater/test work). User can switch the existing tmux client with Ctrl+B then :switch-client -t <name>, no app restart. The root persistence gap for fresh sessions remains a follow-up beyond preserving existing saved IDs.

comments.author: DonutsDelivery
comments.text: Root cause confirmed from user reproduction: fresh Hermes tabs start tmux under the PTY UUID, but restoration previously used the later-discovered Hermes conversation ID as the tmux name. That missed the live tmux session and launched a second --resume process, displaying stale context. Restore now passes the persisted PTY ID as a separate Hermes tmux transport ID while retaining the saved conversation ID for fallback. Regression tests cover reattach transport identity; 63 focused tests and production build pass. Per user request, no live-app restart was performed.

comments.author: DonutsDelivery
comments.text: Second-restart root cause confirmed: after the first correct tmux reattach, workspace persistence replaced the original tmux transport identity with the newly-created PTY client ID. Added persisted transportSessionId across workspace/main/preload/HTTP types, serialization, fresh Hermes tab creation, restore, backend recreation, and crash resume. Legacy tabs migrate from saved ptyId. Added repeated-restore and persistence regressions; 69 focused tests and production build pass. Recovered the live Jak X client to ct-hermes-20260613_223125_7ff5b7 and seeded its running store plus legacy workspace ptyId to 20260613_223125_7ff5b7; verified workspace.json. No app restart performed.

comments.author: DonutsDelivery
comments.text: 2026-07-10 post-cleanup verification: workspace was intentionally empty after all tabs were closed. Hermes state.db still has latest Jak X session 20260613_223125_7ff5b7 (last activity 19:48:19) and Pure Harmony updater session 20260701_001902_e9542e (19:47:28). Session discovery orders by max message timestamp and opening either project without an explicit ID will resume that exact latest durable ID. Tmux is now private/disposable and never used as conversation identity.

comments.author: DonutsDelivery
comments.text: 2026-07-11 root cause confirmed after maximum-crash reproduction: tabs restored, but each launched a wholly unrelated Hermes session because in-TUI /resume changes Hermes's active session ID without updating the app's persisted tab metadata. Implemented per-PTY Hermes runtime directories via TMPDIR, read Hermes's authoritative HERMES_TUI_ACTIVE_SESSION_FILE from pty:list, and synchronize live PTY IDs into workspace persistence every second. This removes title/recency guessing from the identity handoff. Added regression proving stale-session -> selected-by-resume. Focused 70 tests and production build pass; live app was not restarted.

title: Diagnose Hermes segfault and Codex stale update notice

comments.author: DonutsDelivery
comments.text: Diagnosis so far: Hermes segfault is below the terminal layer. Coredumps from both Konsole and Claude-Terminal app executions show /home/user/.hermes/hermes-agent/venv/bin/python3 using pyenv Python 3.11.6 crashing in ordinary CPython paths, including OpenAI SDK responses.create transform, background_review message_sanitization, and slash_worker env loading. This points to Hermes Python runtime/native-extension memory corruption or dependency/runtime mismatch, not xterm/PTY rendering. Codex stale update is a separate PATH/install split: /home/user/.nvm/versions/node/v20.18.1/bin/codex is 0.137.0 while /usr/bin/codex is 0.80.0; app HEAD has uncommitted logic to pick newest Codex, but committed code falls back to PATH.

comments.author: DonutsDelivery
comments.text: Remaining Hermes remediation: likely next step is to stop live Hermes gateway/TUI processes, preserve local Hermes repo changes, and rebuild /home/user/.hermes/hermes-agent/venv with a fresh uv-managed Python/dependency install. Do not do this while active Hermes sessions are running; current host process list still shows gateway, TUI, and MCP watchdog processes under /home/user/.hermes/hermes-agent.

comments.author: DonutsDelivery
comments.text: Codex remediation: verified npm registry latest @openai/codex is 0.144.1. /home/user/.hermes/node/bin/codex was already 0.144.1. Plain shell was launching /home/user/.nvm/versions/node/v20.18.1/bin/codex at 0.137.0; updated that NVM install explicitly with npm --prefix /home/user/.nvm/versions/node/v20.18.1 install -g @openai/codex@latest. Verification: codex, NVM codex, and Hermes-node codex all report 0.144.1. /usr/bin/codex remains 0.80.0 because sudo password is required.

comments.author: DonutsDelivery
comments.text: Resolved and verified: rebuilt Hermes venv on Python 3.12.12 with locked uv dependencies (OpenAI 2.24.0, pydantic 2.13.4, pydantic-core 2.46.4), preserving rollback at /home/user/.hermes/hermes-agent/venv-py311-crash-20260710. hermes --version and the active hermes-gateway.service both use the rebuilt venv; gateway MainPID 1096547 has ExecMainStatus 0. A 2000-iteration threaded OpenAI Responses transform stress test and a clean TUI smoke run passed; no new Hermes/Python core dump appeared. Claude-Terminal fix: treat Hermes as resize-sensitive, suppress default 120x30 output until the settled resize, coalesce startup resizes, skip stale alternate-screen replay and the extra Hermes resize pulse. Fresh runtime verification is clean at 248x62 with suppressOutput false and no scattered characters. Focused pty-manager suite passes 56/56 and production build passes. Full suite still has three unrelated pre-existing failures in session-store fs mocking and theme-name expectations.

title: Research open-source opencode TUI support patterns
description: Compare open-source terminal/AI-code desktop or web apps that support opencode or similar full-screen TUIs, focusing on mouse wheel, xterm.js, PTY, and alternate-screen behavior.
notes: Research summary: Compared OpenCodeUI, 247 Claude Code Remote, cdesktop/Vibe Kanban, wmux, official opencode app, Tether, and Termly. Strongest direct pattern is 247: in alternate buffer/fullscreen TUI mode it sends SGR mouse wheel reports (CSI <64/65;x;yM) to PTY and enables tmux mouse mode. Most xterm peers only forward terminal.onData and rely on xterm local scrollback; none inspected wired terminal.onBinary, despite xterm docs saying some mouse reports use onBinary. OpenCode itself enables TUI mouse capture by default and exposes tui.json scroll_speed/scroll_acceleration/mouse options. Recommended implementation: add terminal.onBinary forwarding, move Ctrl+wheel zoom into attachCustomWheelEventHandler so normal wheel remains xterm-owned, add opencode alternate-buffer fallback to synthesize SGR wheel reports only when xterm/opencode mouse path does not handle it, and consider opencode serve/API integration as longer-term alternative.
close_reason: Research completed; findings recorded and implementation follow-up filed as Claude-Terminal-lge.

title: Add backend-agnostic terminal session crash recovery
description: Across all harness backends, retain a visible failed-tab state and provide safe recovery. Default recovery must resume the tab's persisted backend session identity; Hermes must reattach its persisted tmux transport first. A distinct explicit path must be used for selecting a prior durable session/backup; never silently replace or roll back a conversation. Hermes global state.db snapshots cannot be treated as per-tab backups.

comments.author: DonutsDelivery
comments.text: Implemented the first recovery increment: Terminal forwards PTY exits to tiled tabs; a failed active desktop tab shows an explicit Resume session action. Resume recreates only that tab with its saved backend/session identity, and Hermes additionally uses its persisted tmux transport ID. Focused tests: 65 passed; production build passed. Deliberate recovery from a prior durable session/backup remains open: it must be backend-specific and user-selected, never an automatic global rollback.

comments.author: DonutsDelivery
comments.text: 2026-07-10 safety increment: stopped all 23 detached ct-hermes-* sessions after the user closed all tabs. Hermes now runs in a private tmux server per PTY with a client-detached=kill-server hook, so main-process/PTY loss cannot leave an agent running. Renderer process termination also triggers graceful PTY shutdown. Durable conversation restore remains keyed by Hermes state.db session ID and activity time, separate from disposable tmux transport. Verified isolated tmux hook, 70 focused tests, and production build.

comments.author: DonutsDelivery
comments.text: 2026-07-10 recovery/backup increment: researched official Hermes Desktop, upstream TUI/session docs, and dodo-reach/hermes-desktop. Both resume exact stored IDs and treat state.db as canonical; third-party manager separates transcript inspection from live resume. Official Desktop keeps its hermes serve backend alive across renderer crash/OOM and reloads the renderer with a 3-per-60s crash-loop budget; it prefetches persisted transcript independently before runtime rebind. Neither manager provides automatic rolling state.db recovery snapshots (official exposes only a manual full backup action). Implemented our additional safety layer: SQLite online snapshots before Hermes launch, every 5 minutes while Hermes is active, on renderer gone/unresponsive, emergency Ctrl+Alt+Shift+B, and clean shutdown; retain 3 integrity-checked snapshots outside /tmp. Renderer crash/OOM now snapshots then reloads while backend remains alive. Native Electron WAL backup smoke passed; 71 focused tests and production build pass. App restart intentionally not performed.

comments.author: DonutsDelivery
comments.text: 2026-07-11 forensic correction after OOM/context-loss incident: exact session IDs were insufficient. Four Luna-high read-only investigators established crash-time state. Session 20260710_173016_e497d8 had a confirmed failed HTTP-429 compression that still compacted 643->252 using a lossy deterministic fallback; session 20260711_033613_2a6b0a was killed during compression; stem and Split Notes sessions ended after final patch/background build respectively. Injected evidence-backed recovery handoffs into all four live PTYs, assigned one owner for overlapping Task 5 and constrained the duplicate session to read-only review. Preserved a non-rotating 858MB incident bundle under userData/recovery-incidents/20260711-oom-context-loss with DB, logs, workspace, process/delegation state, and worktree diffs. Backup redesign now separates rolling and incident retention, captures runtime/worktree artifacts on renderer/backend failure, snapshots on Hermes PTY exit, deduplicates burst pre-launch backups, and removes orphan temp/sidecar files. 72 focused tests and production build pass; running app not restarted.

title: Implement OpenCode TUI mouse wheel forwarding
description: Implement the follow-up from Claude-Terminal-izw research: make OpenCode TUI wheel input work in the embedded xterm terminal. Scope: forward xterm onBinary to PTY, move Ctrl+wheel zoom into attachCustomWheelEventHandler so normal wheel events are not blocked, preserve normal-buffer xterm scrollback, and add an OpenCode alternate-buffer fallback that sends SGR wheel reports (CSI <64/65;col;rowM) only when needed. Verify with OpenCode TUI and a mouse-wheel/fullscreen TUI scenario.
acceptance_criteria: 1. xterm onBinary data is forwarded to the PTY path without breaking existing onData input buffering. 2. Ctrl+wheel still changes terminal font size and normal wheel events continue through xterm. 3. OpenCode alternate-screen wheel input scrolls/acts inside the TUI. 4. Normal scrollback wheel behavior still works for non-TUI output. 5. Focused tests or manual verification notes cover the new behavior.
notes: Implemented: xterm custom wheel handler now owns wheel handling, Ctrl+wheel still zooms and returns false to xterm, normal wheel returns true so xterm can process scroll/mouse behavior, onBinary now forwards mouse-report binary data to the PTY, and OpenCode alternate-buffer fallback synthesizes SGR wheel reports only when mouse tracking is not active. Added focused unit tests for Ctrl+wheel, normal scroll passthrough, and fallback SGR output. Verification: npm test -- --run src/renderer/components/terminal/useTerminalSetup/eventHandlers.test.ts passed; npm run build passed; full npm test still has unrelated pre-existing failures in session-store fs mock, pty-manager stale expectations, and theme name expectations.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with focused tests plus production build.

title: Fix Hermes terminal WebGL scroll ghosting
description: Hermes terminal viewport retains old glyphs after scrolling even though xterm's logical buffer is correct. Disable WebGL for Hermes, force a coalesced full-row refresh on xterm scroll, and pin the Electron dev Vite server to port 5173 so restarts cannot open a blank window.

comments.author: DonutsDelivery
comments.text: Reproduced in live Claude-Terminal: webglActive was true and screenshots showed stale glyphs while getTerminalText remained correct. Hermes now uses canvas rendering; the scroll listener refreshes immediately and once on the next animation frame. Repeated injected-scroll cycles are clean with webglActive false. Renderer test passes 3/3, production build passes, and dev Vite is pinned to Electron's fixed 5173 URL.

comments.author: DonutsDelivery
comments.text: Correction: the prior injected-output/programmatic-scroll check did not exercise the user's actual wheel-scroll failure. A direct wheel interception regression also blocked scrolling and was reverted. The issue remains open. Research now shows Hermes peers preserve a live PTY/tmux session and use persisted session history separately; workspace switching must not clear Hermes or replay raw cursor-addressed bytes.

comments.author: DonutsDelivery
comments.text: Root cause refined: direct embedded Hermes --tui redraws corrupt the xterm logical buffer on real wheel scroll. Plain Hermes CLI is stable but loses the TUI session picker/color. Verified outside the sandbox that tmux 3.6b hosting 'hermes --tui --resume 20260613_223125_7ff5b7' renders the full colored TUI and its pane cwd is /home/user/Programs/Jak-X. Implementing tmux-backed Linux Hermes spawning.

comments.author: DonutsDelivery
comments.text: Live tmux verification exposed another concrete defect: screenshot and xterm logical buffer are already corrupted before wheel input. The TUI starts at node-pty's 120x30 default while the tile settles at 248x62; cursor-addressed initial output is then reflowed. Re-enabling Hermes startup suppression will discard that provisional frame and admit only the post-resize redraw.

comments.author: DonutsDelivery
comments.text: Root cause found: handlePtyData stripped TTS/summary/autowork marker characters before writing them to xterm. Hermes's prompt_toolkit full-screen output uses cursor-addressed incremental redraws, so deleting printable bytes desynchronizes its cursor model and produces the observed persistent scattered glyphs in xterm's logical buffer. Full-screen backends now preserve their display byte stream; marker parsing remains side-channel only.

comments.author: DonutsDelivery
comments.text: Validation after raw-stream preservation: live restart screenshot was clean and a real Chromium wheel event (8 steps) left the TUI clean; xterm logical buffer was clean. Follow-up masks each internal marker with equal-width spaces so it remains invisible without changing cursor positions.

comments.author: DonutsDelivery
comments.text: Permission behavior requested: Hermes app invocation now appends the official --yolo flag, including resumed tmux-hosted TUI sessions. Official docs also confirm /yolo toggles this mode in an active chat and HERMES_YOLO_MODE=1 is an environment equivalent.

comments.author: DonutsDelivery
comments.text: Hermes MCP diagnosis during live validation: OpenGOAL project .mcp.json is ignored by Hermes; it must live under ~/.hermes/config.yaml. The disabled python3 entry was replaced with Hermes venv Python and hermes mcp test now discovers all five OpenGOAL tools. Existing chats can run /reload-mcp to refresh MCP tools without restarting Claude-Terminal or the terminal session.

comments.author: DonutsDelivery
comments.text: User confirmed returned text decay is not game-blocking and asked for no app restart. Active live Hermes tmux/xterm dimensions match at 248x61 and Hermes already uses canvas, ruling out initial-size and WebGL paths. Disabled manual scroll-position restoration during alternate-screen TUI clear/redraw frames; focused terminal tests and production build pass. Validation deferred to normal user usage without restarting the app.

title: Implement Grok Build harness support
description: Add Grok Build as a supported terminal harness/backend, including launcher support for grok/agent commands, permission/resume args, CLI checks, instruction file mapping, backend selectors, and HTTP/mobile type plumbing.

title: Research opencode TUI scrollwheel support improvements
description: Investigate how Claude Terminal can improve opencode TUI mouse/scrollwheel support, including xterm.js behavior, mouse tracking, and implementation options.
notes: Research findings: opencode runs as a full-screen/mouse-reporting TUI, so scrollwheel support should be delivered as terminal mouse wheel escape sequences, not browser scrollback. Current renderer attaches a DOM wheel listener only for zoom + userScrolledUp tracking and does not use xterm attachCustomWheelEventHandler or onBinary. xterm.js supports mouse tracking modes and SGR mouse mode; its onBinary event is specifically for binary mouse reports that must be passed to the PTY as binary data. Recommended direction: let xterm process normal wheel events, use attachCustomWheelEventHandler only to intercept Ctrl+wheel zoom, wire terminal.onBinary to pty write path, and add a backend-aware fallback for opencode/alternate-screen when mouseTrackingMode is none by sending repeated ArrowUp/ArrowDown or SGR wheel events. Need validation with opencode TUI.
close_reason: Research completed; recommendations captured in task notes and final handoff.

title: Fix stale TUI render on tab/workspace switch
description: When switching tabs or workspaces, Ink-based TUIs (Claude Code, OpenCode) stay in a stale render state — only newly written cells repaint, the rest of the framebuffer is blank. Manual tile/window resize fixes it because the resulting ResizeObserver fire calls handleResize which (a) re-fits the terminal and (b) sends a real SIGWINCH with the new dimensions. The fix is to do the same transient SIGWINCH (rows-1 then back to rows) that's already in terminalInit.ts postOpenSetup, but also when a terminal transitions from inactive→active. Edit src/renderer/components/terminal/Terminal.tsx isActive useEffect to do the transient SIGWINCH.

title: Revert headroom user-message compression toggle
notes: Diagnosed intermittent Hermes API failures: MiniMax headroom proxy on 127.0.0.1:8788 was disappearing after a Python native SIGSEGV in headroom/ONNX/tokenizer/OpenBLAS stack. Fix removes Hermes MINIMAX_BASE_URL headroom routing, stops app from launching the MiniMax proxy, and removes HEADROOM_COMPRESS_USER_MESSAGES/aggressive threshold env vars from headroom-proxy. Temporary restartable 8788 systemd-run service is active only to support already-running Hermes sessions.
close_reason: Fixed: reverted user-message/aggressive headroom envs, stopped app from launching the MiniMax headroom proxy, and stopped new Hermes PTYs from overriding MINIMAX_BASE_URL. Focused PTY tests and production build pass.

title: Restore Claude connectivity after headroom proxy outage
notes: Live diagnosis: Claude sessions had ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8787 but no process was listening on 8787, causing connection refused. Restored 8787 as restartable user service without HEADROOM_COMPRESS_USER_MESSAGES/HEADROOM_MIN_TOKENS/HEADROOM_INTERCEPT_READ_MIN_CHARS. Disabled headroomEnabled in saved config and via running app debug bridge so new PTYs do not route through headroom.
close_reason: Restored live Claude connectivity: 8787 bridge is healthy and restartable for existing sessions; headroom disabled in running app and saved config for future sessions.
